goal: Yrkesroll: MIG welder (ONET SOC 51-4121, Welders, Cutters, Solderers, and Brazers). A UR10 holds a welding torch (cylinder tool attached to the gripper via pick_place pickup from a torch stand) and performs FOUR discrete tack welds along the seam between two metal sheet halves (Cube prims placed edge-to-edge on a workpiece fixture). Each tack = move_to_pose above tack location → dwell stationary for ~0.5 s of sim-time → spawn a small visual marker Cube (the 'fused-spot' representation) at the tack position → advance to next tack location. No arc/fusion physics: the dwell + marker-spawn pair is the structural representation of a tack weld. Realises backlog item yrkesroll-welder-mig-tack-001. Distinct from CP-NEW-welder-seam-track (CONTINUOUS impedance-compliant seam follow with vision correction along a curved weld pass) by the DISCRETE-DWELL pattern: stationary tack-hold-advance instead of continuous trajectory traversal, no impedance/admittance compliance, no vision_plan_trajectory correction loop.
thoughts:
Design rationale for CP-NEW-yrkesroll-welder-mig-tack:

1) Yrkesroll framing — MIG welder per ONET SOC 51-4121 (Welders, Cutters, Solderers, and Brazers) per backlog entry yrkesroll-welder-mig-tack-001. The backlog calls for a 4-point tack-weld trajectory; this template ships the DISCRETE rendering (move → dwell → marker → repeat) rather than the continuous follow-trajectory rendering shipped by CP-NEW-welder-seam-track (yrkesroll-welder-seam-track-002). Tack welding is a real industrial pre-step before full seam welding — short stationary welds that fixture two sheets together so the full seam pass can run without the sheets shifting. Modelling it as a distinct discrete pattern keeps the seam-track canonical's continuous-compliance semantics clean and gives downstream retrieval a clean discriminator (isaac:gesture.tack_weld + isaac:gesture.discrete_dwell vs isaac:topology.seam_tracking + isaac:motion.compliant_follow).

2) DISCRETE vs CONTINUOUS — the critical discriminator from CP-NEW-welder-seam-track:
   - A28 (seam-track): single follow_trajectory_with_compliance call traversing 4 waypoints continuously with impedance compliance + Z-softness for torch contact pressure; vision_plan_trajectory corrects path mid-flight. The robot is moving the WHOLE time.
   - A95 (tack): 4 separate move_to_pose calls, each followed by sim_control(action='step', step_count=30) for a ~0.5 s stationary dwell during which a marker Cube is spawned. No compliance controller, no vision correction, no Z-softness. The robot is STATIONARY during each tack and moves only between tacks.
   The semantic tags isaac:gesture.tack_weld and isaac:gesture.discrete_dwell encode this discrimination at retrieval time.

3) Topology — single station on a 1.0×0.8×0.75m work table at origin. UR10 at [0, 0, 0.75]. Workpiece fixture (a flat Cube clamp) at [0.50, 0.0, 0.78] holding two thin sheet halves (Cube prims, scale [0.10, 0.20, 0.005]) edge-to-edge along the x-axis, with a small 0.003m gap between them — that gap IS the seam to be tacked. Sheet left at [0.45, 0.0, 0.785] (x = 0.40 .. 0.50 edge); sheet right at [0.55, 0.0, 0.785] (x = 0.50 .. 0.60 edge); seam centre at x=0.50. Torch stand at [0.20, 0.30, 0.80] (a flat platform holding the welding torch cylinder so the robot can pick it up). All within UR10 1.3m reach envelope.

4) Welding torch — a Cylinder prim representing the MIG torch tip. Length 0.12 m, radius 0.015 m, vertical axis, stashed on the torch stand at [0.20, 0.30, 0.86] (centre z = stand_top + tool_length/2). Mass 0.30 kg (light enough for parallel-jaw side grip on the torch handle). define_grasp_pose installed: approach_direction = [-1, 0, 0] (gripper approaches from -x side, picking the torch in vertical orientation), gripper_offset = [0, 0, 0]. The torch is treated as a regular liftable rigid body; once picked up the gripper carries it through the 4 tack-pose move_to_pose calls. After the final tack the torch is NOT returned to the stand (the work-cycle ends with the torch held aloft; future-variant could include a return-to-stand phase).

5) Four tack positions — along the seam (y-axis, since seam runs in y-direction at x=0.50, between the two sheets which span y = -0.10 .. +0.10). Tack pitch chosen so the four spots are evenly distributed along the seam length: y = -0.075, -0.025, +0.025, +0.075 (4 spots, 0.05 m centre-to-centre, leaving 0.025 m clearance at each seam end). Tack pose: position [0.50, y_tack, 0.835] (torch tip ~0.025 m above seam surface to leave standoff for the simulated 'arc'); orientation [0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068] (torch pointing straight down with the standard top-down approach quaternion used throughout the repo).

6) Discrete-dwell pattern in code — for each of the 4 tacks:
   (a) move_to_pose(target_position=[0.50, y_tack, 0.835], target_orientation=...) — sends the UR10 to the tack pose.
   (b) sim_control(action='step', step_count=30) — 30 sim-steps @ 60 Hz = ~0.5 s stationary dwell. This is where the 'arc' would heat metal. No physics happens; the dwell is structural.
   (c) create_prim(prim_path=f'/World/TackMarker_{i}', prim_type='Cube', position=[0.50, y_tack, 0.788], size=0.006) — spawn the 'fused-spot' marker at the tack location, slightly above the sheet top (z=0.7875) so it sits visibly proud. Marker is a static visual cube, no rigid body, no collision.
   (d) set_attribute(prim_path=..., attr_name='primvars:displayColor', value=[[0.95, 0.6, 0.05]]) — orange-yellow fused-metal colour.
   The marker spawn is what gives downstream tools (verify_args / vision_analyze_scene at scenario level) a structural signal that a tack 'happened'.

7) Setup pick-place for torch pickup — a single setup_pick_place_controller call brings the torch from the stand to the first tack pose. source_paths=[/World/Torch], destination_path=/World/Sheet_Left (anchor for the first tack-approach), drop_targets={/World/Torch: [0.50, -0.075, 0.835]} (= first tack pose). After this call the robot is at tack 1 with torch held. The remaining 3 tacks use move_to_pose only (no controller re-bind needed — the gripper stays closed on the torch).

8) Physics — CPU deterministic (enable_gpu_dynamics=False, broadphase_type=MBP). Two sheets have PhysicsRigidBodyAPI + PhysicsCollisionAPI + low mass (0.20 kg each) + 'metal' physics material for high friction-on-fixture; they could shift slightly during torch contact in a real weld but here they are static-by-mass (the robot does not contact them — tack pose is 0.025 m above the surface). Fixture has PhysicsCollisionAPI only, no RigidBodyAPI (static obstacle). Torch has full rigid-body schemas so it can be picked up. TackMarker prims have NO rigid-body schemas (visual-only).

9) Semantic labels — Sheet_Left + Sheet_Right tagged 'metal_sheet' (class='workpiece_sheet'); Torch tagged 'mig_torch' (class='welding_tool'); each TackMarker_N tagged 'tack_weld_spot' (class='weld_spot'). The class='weld_spot' label is the semantic signal a scenario-profile vision_analyze_scene call would key off to count tacks.

10) Distinct structural tags from CP-NEW-welder-seam-track:
   - isaac:yrkesroll.welder (shared — both are welder yrkesrolls)
   - isaac:industry.mig_welding (NEW — distinct from seam_tracking; MIG-specific)
   - isaac:gesture.tack_weld (NEW — primary discriminator; spot-style discrete weld)
   - isaac:gesture.discrete_dwell (NEW — secondary discriminator; stationary-hold pattern)
   - isaac:operation.spot_weld_sequence (NEW — emphasises the 4-spot multi-marker sequence)
   - isaac:robot.ur10, isaac:robot.fixed_base.arm, isaac:topology.single_station (shared shape)
   ABSENT: isaac:compliance.impedance, isaac:motion.compliant_follow, isaac:motion.vision_corrected, isaac:topology.seam_tracking, isaac:sensor.vision_plan — these are seam-track-only.

11) Verification — verify_args.stages has a single stage (the torch pickup pick-place); the 4 tack moves are not pick-places so they live in simulate_args via duration_s budget. simulate_args.tack_count=4, simulate_args.target_path=/World/Sheet_Left (the seam anchor); simulate_args.marker_path_prefix='/World/TackMarker_' enables a counting check. diagnose_args includes the fixture + both sheets + the torch stand as obstacles.

12) Failure modes (specific to discrete-dwell pattern, not seam-track):
   - Tack position drift: cuRobo IK lands the torch tip 5-10 mm off the seam centre x=0.50. Mitigation: tighten position tolerance via explicit motion-planner xy_tolerance, OR add a post-move get_world_transform check that re-issues move_to_pose if drift > 5 mm.
   - Dwell too short to 'fuse': if sim_control step_count is reduced below ~10, the marker spawn may happen before the simulated dwell completes — downstream vision counters might see the marker but miss the dwell semantics. Mitigation: keep step_count=30 (= 0.5 s) as a structural floor; any scenario-profile that wants longer dwell should override at instance-build time.
   - Torch lifts during dwell (gravity gap): if the gripper attach is loose, gravity pulls the 0.30 kg torch downward during the 0.5 s stationary dwell, causing the tack-pose torch position to drift by 1-2 mm. Mitigation: set Franka/UR10 parallel-jaw force_grasp parameter when picking, or upgrade to surface_gripper tool for guaranteed attach. Not exercised here.
   - Seam misalignment: if the two sheets are placed with the seam gap >0.005 m (vs designed 0.003 m), the tack markers spawn correctly but the 'tacked' shape does not match the programmed seam — sheets could still shift under the robot. Mitigation: tighten sheet placement tolerance OR add a PhysicsFixedJoint between Sheet_Left and the fixture at build time.
   - Marker spawn placed below sheet top: if marker z is set to sheet centre (0.785) instead of slightly above (0.788), the marker visual interpenetrates the sheet and renders incorrectly. Mitigation: z_marker = z_sheet_top + marker_size/2 + 0.001 clearance.

13) Comparison to siblings:
   - vs CP-NEW-welder-seam-track (A28): A28 = continuous compliant follow + vision; A95 = discrete dwell + marker spawn. Different motion controller stack, different gesture tags.
   - vs CP-NEW-yrkesroll-machinist-cnc-load (A74): both are yrkesroll templates with operator_role metadata; CNC is a multi-phase pick-place + door-cycle, A95 is single-phase pick + multi-move dwell-spot sequence.
   - vs CP-NEW-yrkesroll-packer-box-seal (A88): A88 = two pick-place phases (pack + lid); A95 = one pick-place phase + 4 move_to_pose tacks. Different structural-features signature (has_seal_phase vs n_tack_spots).

14) motion_controllers: untested = curobo (used by setup_pick_place_controller target_source) + rmpflow (default move_to_pose planner). No compliance controllers needed.
code:
# ── Scene skeleton ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# Robot work table (1.0 × 0.8 × 0.75m)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.5, 0.4, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# ── Physics scene: CPU dynamics for determinism ───────────────────────────────
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# ── UR10 robot at table edge ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="ur10",
    dest_path="/World/UR10",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[1, 0, 0, 0],
)

# ── Workpiece fixture (flat clamp platform) ───────────────────────────────────
# Top z = 0.78. Sheets rest at z = 0.785 (centre, 0.005m thick).
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/WorkpieceFixture",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.50, 0.0, 0.78],
    scale=[0.20, 0.25, 0.01],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/WorkpieceFixture", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/WorkpieceFixture", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.30, 0.30, 0.35]])

# ── Two metal sheet halves — seam between them at x=0.50, running in y ────────
# Sheet_Left: x = 0.40 .. 0.50 (centre x=0.45), span y = -0.10 .. +0.10.
# Sheet_Right: x = 0.50 .. 0.60 (centre x=0.55), span y = -0.10 .. +0.10.
# Thickness 0.005m → centre z = 0.785 (sheet bottom = 0.7825, sheet top = 0.7875).
# The 0.003m gap modelled by setting sheet centres 0.10 apart instead of 0.0975 — the
# nominal 0.10 gap-centre-to-centre with 0.10 scale gives edges meeting at 0.50 exactly;
# we offset by 0.0015 on each side to leave a 0.003m air gap (the seam).
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Sheet_Left",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.4485, 0.0, 0.785],
    scale=[0.10, 0.20, 0.005],
)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Sheet_Left", schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Sheet_Left", attr_name="physics:mass", value=0.20)
apply_physics_material(prim_path="/World/Sheet_Left", material_name="metal")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Sheet_Left", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.65, 0.65, 0.70]])
set_semantic_label(prim_path="/World/Sheet_Left", label="metal_sheet", class_name="workpiece_sheet")

create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Sheet_Right",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.5515, 0.0, 0.785],
    scale=[0.10, 0.20, 0.005],
)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Sheet_Right", schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Sheet_Right", attr_name="physics:mass", value=0.20)
apply_physics_material(prim_path="/World/Sheet_Right", material_name="metal")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Sheet_Right", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.65, 0.65, 0.70]])
set_semantic_label(prim_path="/World/Sheet_Right", label="metal_sheet", class_name="workpiece_sheet")

bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=["/World/Sheet_Left", "/World/Sheet_Right"],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)

# ── Torch stand (flat platform holding the MIG torch) ─────────────────────────
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/TorchStand",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.20, 0.30, 0.78],
    scale=[0.08, 0.08, 0.01],
)
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/TorchStand", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/TorchStand", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.25, 0.25, 0.30]])

# ── MIG welding torch (cylinder tool, picked up by the robot) ─────────────────
# Vertical cylinder, length 0.12m, radius 0.015m. Centre z = stand_top(0.79) + 0.06 = 0.85.
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/Torch",
    prim_type="Cylinder",
    position=[0.20, 0.30, 0.85],
    scale=[0.015, 0.015, 0.06],
)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Torch", schema_name=api)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Torch", attr_name="physics:mass", value=0.30)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/Torch", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.85, 0.80, 0.20]])
set_semantic_label(prim_path="/World/Torch", label="mig_torch", class_name="welding_tool")

# Top-down approach grasp pose for the torch handle (cylinder is vertical).
define_grasp_pose(
    robot_path="/World/UR10",
    object_path="/World/Torch",
    approach_direction=[0, 0, -1],
    gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0],
)

# ── PHASE 1: pick the torch from the stand and bring it to tack-1 pose ────────
# Tack 1 pose: [0.50, -0.075, 0.835] — first of four spots along the seam.
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/UR10",
    target_source="curobo",
    robot_family="ur10",
    source_paths=["/World/Torch"],
    destination_path="/World/Sheet_Left",
    drop_targets={
        "/World/Torch": [0.50, -0.075, 0.835],
    },
    planning_obstacles=[
        "/World/Table",
        "/World/TorchStand",
        "/World/WorkpieceFixture",
        "/World/Sheet_Left",
        "/World/Sheet_Right",
    ],
)

# Verify torch reached tack-1 pose.
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Torch")

# ── PHASE 2: discrete-dwell sequence — 4 tack welds along the seam ────────────
# For each tack: (a) move_to_pose to tack location, (b) dwell for ~0.5s sim-time,
# (c) spawn visual marker representing the fused spot, (d) advance to next.

# Tack 1 (already at this pose from phase 1) — dwell + marker only.
sim_control(action="step", step_count=30)
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/TackMarker_1",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.50, -0.075, 0.788],
    size=0.006,
)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/TackMarker_1", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.95, 0.60, 0.05]])
set_semantic_label(prim_path="/World/TackMarker_1", label="tack_weld_spot", class_name="weld_spot")

# Tack 2.
move_to_pose(
    articulation_path="/World/UR10",
    target_position=[0.50, -0.025, 0.835],
    target_orientation=[0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068],,
    robot_type="ur10"
)
sim_control(action="step", step_count=30)
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/TackMarker_2",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.50, -0.025, 0.788],
    size=0.006,
)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/TackMarker_2", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.95, 0.60, 0.05]])
set_semantic_label(prim_path="/World/TackMarker_2", label="tack_weld_spot", class_name="weld_spot")

# Tack 3.
move_to_pose(
    articulation_path="/World/UR10",
    target_position=[0.50, 0.025, 0.835],
    target_orientation=[0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068],,
    robot_type="ur10"
)
sim_control(action="step", step_count=30)
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/TackMarker_3",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.50, 0.025, 0.788],
    size=0.006,
)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/TackMarker_3", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.95, 0.60, 0.05]])
set_semantic_label(prim_path="/World/TackMarker_3", label="tack_weld_spot", class_name="weld_spot")

# Tack 4.
move_to_pose(
    articulation_path="/World/UR10",
    target_position=[0.50, 0.075, 0.835],
    target_orientation=[0.0, 0.7071068, 0.0, 0.7071068],,
    robot_type="ur10"
)
sim_control(action="step", step_count=30)
create_prim(
    prim_path="/World/TackMarker_4",
    prim_type="Cube",
    position=[0.50, 0.075, 0.788],
    size=0.006,
)
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/TackMarker_4", attr_name="primvars:displayColor", value=[[0.95, 0.60, 0.05]])
set_semantic_label(prim_path="/World/TackMarker_4", label="tack_weld_spot", class_name="weld_spot")

# Final verification — torch should be at tack-4 pose, all 4 markers present.
get_world_transform(prim_path="/World/Torch")
failure_modes: Tack position drift — cuRobo IK lands the torch tip 5-10 mm off seam centre x=0.50. The discrete-dwell pattern has no compliance to absorb position error (unlike A28 seam-track which has Z-soft impedance). Mitigation: tighten move_to_pose tolerance via planner xy_tolerance arg, OR add a get_world_transform check after each move_to_pose and re-issue move_to_pose with corrected target if drift > 5 mm.

Dwell time too short to 'fuse' — if sim_control(action='step', step_count=N) is reduced below ~10, the marker spawn may happen effectively in parallel with the torch arrival, breaking the dwell-then-spawn semantic ordering. The 0.5 s dwell at step_count=30 is the structural floor. Downstream scenario-profile verifiers that check expected_dwell_steps_per_tack will mark the canonical as semantically incomplete if step_count is lowered.

Torch lifts during dwell (gravity gap) — if the gripper-torch attach is loose, gravity pulls the 0.30 kg torch downward during the 0.5 s stationary dwell, drifting the tack-pose torch position by 1-2 mm. The torch is a regular rigid body picked by parallel-jaw grip (not a fixed-joint attach). Mitigation: increase parallel-jaw grip force when picking, or upgrade to surface_gripper for a guaranteed kinematic attach. Not exercised here.

Seam misalignment — sheets placed with seam gap >0.005 m (vs designed 0.003 m) shifts the actual joint location away from x=0.50. The 4 tack markers spawn at the programmed pose, but they no longer span the real seam. Mitigation: tighten sheet-placement role_defaults tolerance OR add PhysicsFixedJoint between each Sheet_{Left,Right} and the WorkpieceFixture so the sheets cannot shift after build.

Marker spawn placed below sheet top — sheet top z=0.7875, marker centre z=0.788, marker size 0.006 → marker bottom = 0.785 (= sheet centre = 0.0005 m below sheet top). If marker size grows to 0.010 the bottom interpenetrates the sheet. Mitigation: keep z_marker = z_sheet_top + marker_size/2 + 0.001 clearance.

DISCRETE vs CONTINUOUS confusion at retrieval — A28 (CP-NEW-welder-seam-track) and A95 (this template) both implement ONET SOC 51-4121 welder yrkesroll; an embedding-based retriever might collapse them. The structural_tags isaac:gesture.tack_weld + isaac:gesture.discrete_dwell are the primary discriminators against isaac:topology.seam_tracking + isaac:motion.compliant_follow. If a user asks for 'tack welds' or 'spot welds', the gesture.tack_weld tag must win; if they ask for 'seam follow' or 'continuous weld', the topology.seam_tracking tag wins.

No PhysicsCollisionAPI on Torch when picked — once the torch is grasped, cuRobo by default does NOT update the torch's collision body to follow the gripper, so the torch-cylinder geometry is checked against itself at the pre-pick pose and against the new tack pose during planning, which can trigger spurious collision rejection. Mitigation handled by setup_pick_place_controller's attach-payload-as-end-effector mechanism (target_source='curobo' enables it). If using a different motion stack this must be wired explicitly.

Sheet rigid-body settle disturbs seam — sheets have RigidBodyAPI + low mass (0.20 kg) and rest on the fixture by gravity. During the ~0.5 s dwell steps the sheets are not actively held; if the fixture has imperfect contact friction the sheets may shift by 1-3 mm under their own weight in the dwell window, drifting the seam under the parked torch. Mitigation: add PhysicsFixedJoint between each sheet and the fixture, OR raise sleepThreshold so sheets stop simulating after the build phase.

tack_markers.note: Spawned at runtime, one per tack pose. Default 4 spots evenly distributed along the seam.

operator_role.title: MIG Welder (Tack-Weld Specialist)
operator_role.onet_soc_note: 51-4121 is the direct ONET match for manual MIG/MAG/TIG welding including tack-weld pre-fixturing before full seam welding. Adjacent code 51-4122 (Welding, Soldering, and Brazing Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders) applies to automated welding-machine tending and is preferred when the workflow is machine-operator-centric rather than hands-on torch work; 51-4121 used here for the hands-on tack-weld workflow with robot assist.

code_template:
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[0.5, 0.4, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})
robot_wizard(robot_name={{primary_robot.class}}, dest_path={{primary_robot.path}}, position={{primary_robot.position}}, orientation={{primary_robot.orientation}})
create_prim(prim_path={{workpiece_fixture.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{workpiece_fixture.position}}, scale={{workpiece_fixture.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{workpiece_fixture.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path={{sheet_left.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{sheet_left.position}}, scale={{sheet_left.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{sheet_left.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsRigidBodyAPI")
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{sheet_left.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{sheet_left.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsMassAPI")
set_attribute(prim_path={{sheet_left.path}}, attr_name="physics:mass", value={{sheet_left.mass}})
apply_physics_material(prim_path={{sheet_left.path}}, material_name={{sheet_left.material}})
set_semantic_label(prim_path={{sheet_left.path}}, label={{sheet_left.label}}, class_name="workpiece_sheet")
create_prim(prim_path={{sheet_right.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{sheet_right.position}}, scale={{sheet_right.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{sheet_right.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsRigidBodyAPI")
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{sheet_right.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{sheet_right.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsMassAPI")
set_attribute(prim_path={{sheet_right.path}}, attr_name="physics:mass", value={{sheet_right.mass}})
apply_physics_material(prim_path={{sheet_right.path}}, material_name={{sheet_right.material}})
set_semantic_label(prim_path={{sheet_right.path}}, label={{sheet_right.label}}, class_name="workpiece_sheet")
bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=[{{sheet_left.path}}, {{sheet_right.path}}], attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)
create_prim(prim_path={{torch_stand.path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{torch_stand.position}}, scale={{torch_stand.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{torch_stand.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path={{torch.path}}, prim_type="Cylinder", position={{torch.position}}, scale={{torch.scale}})
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{torch.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsRigidBodyAPI")
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{torch.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
apply_api_schema(prim_path={{torch.path}}, schema_name="PhysicsMassAPI")
set_attribute(prim_path={{torch.path}}, attr_name="physics:mass", value={{torch.mass}})
set_semantic_label(prim_path={{torch.path}}, label={{torch.label}}, class_name="welding_tool")
define_grasp_pose(robot_path={{primary_robot.path}}, object_path={{torch.path}}, approach_direction={{torch.approach_direction}}, gripper_offset=[0, 0, 0])
setup_pick_place_controller(robot_path={{primary_robot.path}}, target_source="curobo", robot_family={{primary_robot.family}}, source_paths=[{{torch.path}}], destination_path={{sheet_left.path}}, drop_targets={ {{torch.path}}: {{tack_poses[0].position}} }, planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", {{torch_stand.path}}, {{workpiece_fixture.path}}, {{sheet_left.path}}, {{sheet_right.path}}])
get_world_transform(prim_path={{torch.path}})
sim_control(action="step", step_count={{dwell_step_count}})
create_prim(prim_path={{tack_poses[0].marker_path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{tack_poses[0].marker_position}}, size={{marker_size}})
set_semantic_label(prim_path={{tack_poses[0].marker_path}}, label="tack_weld_spot", class_name="weld_spot")
move_to_pose(articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}}, target_position={{tack_poses[1].position}}, target_orientation={{tack_orientation}}, robot_type="ur10")
sim_control(action="step", step_count={{dwell_step_count}})
create_prim(prim_path={{tack_poses[1].marker_path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{tack_poses[1].marker_position}}, size={{marker_size}})
set_semantic_label(prim_path={{tack_poses[1].marker_path}}, label="tack_weld_spot", class_name="weld_spot")
move_to_pose(articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}}, target_position={{tack_poses[2].position}}, target_orientation={{tack_orientation}}, robot_type="ur10")
sim_control(action="step", step_count={{dwell_step_count}})
create_prim(prim_path={{tack_poses[2].marker_path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{tack_poses[2].marker_position}}, size={{marker_size}})
set_semantic_label(prim_path={{tack_poses[2].marker_path}}, label="tack_weld_spot", class_name="weld_spot")
move_to_pose(articulation_path={{primary_robot.path}}, target_position={{tack_poses[3].position}}, target_orientation={{tack_orientation}}, robot_type="ur10")
sim_control(action="step", step_count={{dwell_step_count}})
create_prim(prim_path={{tack_poses[3].marker_path}}, prim_type="Cube", position={{tack_poses[3].marker_position}}, size={{marker_size}})
set_semantic_label(prim_path={{tack_poses[3].marker_path}}, label="tack_weld_spot", class_name="weld_spot")
get_world_transform(prim_path={{torch.path}})
extension_notes: Implements ONET SOC 51-4121 MIG welder yrkesroll via the DISCRETE-DWELL pattern (4 stationary tack welds along a seam between two metal sheets) — distinct from sibling CP-NEW-welder-seam-track which implements the CONTINUOUS impedance-compliant seam-follow pattern for the same ONET code. Backlog item yrkesroll-welder-mig-tack-001 calls for record_waypoints + follow_trajectory_with_compliance; this template ships the simpler discrete-move + dwell + marker-spawn rendering that matches the user's pattern_hint='other' classification (tack welding = pause + spark + pause = neither pick_place nor continuous trajectory). Discriminator tags: isaac:gesture.tack_weld + isaac:gesture.discrete_dwell vs A28's isaac:topology.seam_tracking + isaac:motion.compliant_follow. Real arc/fusion physics are not modelled: each dwell-then-marker-spawn pair is the structural representation of a tack. Failure modes call out the lack of compliance vs A28 — tack position drift cannot be absorbed by Z-soft impedance here.
